walkthrough.title: Choose Your Galaxy
walkthrough.text: Welcome, Commander! Your mission to conquer the cosmos begins here. Give each player a Galaxy Mat in their chosen color. The Galaxy Mat is your home base -- it tracks your empire level, resources, and houses your fleet. Make sure the mat is placed with the normal side face up; the Rogue Galaxy side on the back is only used for solo play.

walkthrough.title: Gather Your Fleet and Tokens
walkthrough.text: Each player takes 4 ships, 1 Culture token, 1 Energy token, and 1 Empire token in their matching color. These tiny rockets are your fleet -- they will explore distant planets, gather resources, and stake your claim across the galaxy. Keep everything organized near your Galaxy Mat.

walkthrough.title: Deploy Starting Ships
walkthrough.text: Place 2 of your ships standing upright on the center of your Galaxy Mat. These are your active fleet, ready for deployment. The other 2 ships go on the ship track, placed on the numbered spaces outlined with a square. You will unlock these additional ships as your empire grows.

walkthrough.title: Set Starting Resources
walkthrough.text: Place your Culture token on the 1 space and your Energy token on the 2 space of the resource track on your Galaxy Mat. Energy fuels rerolls and empire upgrades, while Culture lets you follow other players' actions. Place your Empire token on the first space of the empire track, marked with a star icon. Your empire level determines how many dice and ships you can use each turn.

walkthrough.title: Discover New Planets
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the entire deck of Planet Cards. Draw a number of planets equal to the number of players plus two, and lay them face up in a line in the center of the table. In a 5-player game, only draw 6 planets. These are the newly discovered worlds ready for colonization -- each one shows a point value, a special action, and a colony track.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Control Mat and Dice
walkthrough.text: Place the Control Mat in the center of the table alongside the 7 Action Dice. The Control Mat has an Activation Bay where used dice are placed, and a Converter section for transforming dice. All players will share these dice and this mat throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Deal Secret Missions
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the Secret Mission cards and deal 2 to each player. Look at both cards, choose one to keep, and slide it face down under your Galaxy Mat. Discard the other card. Your Secret Mission is a hidden objective that awards bonus victory points at the end of the game -- keep it a secret from your rivals! Return any remaining mission cards to the box.

bullets: Each player takes a Galaxy Mat in their chosen color
Use the normal side (Rogue Galaxy on back is for solo play only)

summary.title: Gather Your Fleet and Tokens
bullets: 4 ships, 1 Culture token, 1 Energy token, 1 Empire token per player
All in matching color

summary.title: Deploy Starting Ships
bullets: 2 ships standing upright on center of Galaxy Mat (active fleet)
2 ships on ship track on squared number spaces (locked until upgraded)

summary.title: Set Starting Resources
bullets: Culture token on 1, Energy token on 2 on resource track
Empire token on first star space of empire track

summary.title: Discover New Planets
bullets: Shuffle Planet Cards, draw (# players + 2) face up in center
5-player game: only draw 6 planets

summary.title: Prepare the Control Mat and Dice
bullets: Control Mat with Activation Bay and Converter in center
Place all 7 Action Dice nearby

summary.title: Deal Secret Missions
bullets: Deal 2 Secret Mission cards per player, keep 1, discard the other
Slide chosen card face down under Galaxy Mat -- secret until endgame

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview and Rolling Dice
walkthrough.text: The youngest player takes the first turn. On your turn, you take the number of dice shown on your empire track and roll them all at once. At empire level 1, that is 4 dice. The faces you roll determine which actions you can take this turn. You may activate your dice in any order, and you do not have to use all of them.

walkthrough.title: Activating Dice
walkthrough.text: To use a die, move it to the Activation Bay on the Control Mat and perform its action. Once a die is placed in the Activation Bay, it is spent -- you cannot use it again this turn. Think carefully about which dice to activate first, because the order can matter strategically.

walkthrough.title: Rerolling Dice
walkthrough.text: At any point during your turn, you may reroll any number of your unactivated dice. Your first reroll each turn is free. Every reroll after that costs 1 Energy. This means you can fish for the exact action you need, but it will drain your fuel reserves. Dice already in the Activation Bay cannot be rerolled.

walkthrough.title: Move a Ship
walkthrough.text: The Move action lets you relocate one of your ships from your Galaxy Mat or any planet to a different planet. When your ship arrives, you choose one of two options: land on the planet surface (standing upright) to immediately perform that planet's special action, or enter orbit on the colony track (lying on its side) to begin working toward colonization.

walkthrough.title: Movement Rules
walkthrough.text: When moving, you must switch planets -- you cannot move from a planet's surface to its colony track or vice versa on the same planet. You may have one ship on a planet's surface and one on its colony track. Ships from different players can share the same spaces. You may return a ship to your Galaxy Mat. You can never land on another player's Galaxy Mat or their colonized planets.

walkthrough.title: Acquire Resources: Energy and Culture
walkthrough.text: There are two Acquire Resource dice faces: Energy and Culture. When you activate an Acquire Energy die, gain 1 Energy for each of your ships on or orbiting planets with the Energy symbol, plus 1 Energy for each ship on your Galaxy Mat. Acquire Culture works the same way but for planets with the Culture symbol -- ships on your Galaxy Mat do NOT produce Culture. Resources max out at 7 each.

walkthrough.title: Advance Colonization: Diplomacy and Economy
walkthrough.text: Diplomacy and Economy dice advance your ships along colony tracks. Each planet's colony track ends with either a Diplomacy or Economy symbol, which determines which die type advances ships on that track. Activate the matching die to move one of your orbiting ships forward one space on one colony track.

walkthrough.title: Colonizing a Planet
walkthrough.text: When your ship reaches the final space of a colony track, you have colonized that planet. All ships on that planet card -- yours and opponents' -- are returned to their owners' Galaxy Mats. Take the planet card and slide it under the action section of your Galaxy Mat so the text and point value remain visible. Draw a new planet to replace it. Announce your updated victory point total.

walkthrough.title: Utilize a Colony
walkthrough.text: The Utilize a Colony die lets you perform either the action printed on your Galaxy Mat or the action on any one planet you have colonized. Your Galaxy Mat action is always 'Upgrade Your Empire' -- spend resources equal to the next empire level (paid entirely in Energy or entirely in Culture, never mixed). Upgrading increases your VP, dice count, and may unlock new ships.

walkthrough.title: The Converter
walkthrough.text: Once per turn, you may use the Converter on the Control Mat. Place any two unactivated dice into the Converter slots -- these dice are spent and cannot be activated or followed. Then change a third unactivated die to any face of your choice. This is a powerful tool for getting exactly the action you need when the dice are not cooperating.

walkthrough.title: Following Other Players
walkthrough.text: After the active player activates a die, every other player gets a brief window to follow that action by spending 1 Culture. A following player performs the exact same action as if they had activated that die themselves. Each player may only follow once per die. Timing disputes are resolved clockwise from the active player. Following keeps you engaged on every single turn.

walkthrough.title: End of Game and Scoring
walkthrough.text: When any player reaches 21 or more victory points, the endgame is triggered. Play continues until all players have taken an equal number of turns (the starting player does not take another turn). Then all players reveal their Secret Mission cards and add any bonus VP earned. Total your points from colonized planets, empire level, and secret mission. Highest total wins! Ties are broken by most colonized planets, then highest empire level, then most combined resources.

walkthrough.title: Recommended Player Count for Learning
walkthrough.text: For your first voyage into Tiny Epic Galaxies, we recommend 2-3 players. This keeps the game tight, lets everyone see how following works, and keeps downtime low. A 2-player game runs about 25 minutes, giving you time to play twice and really internalize the mechanics.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Training Mission
walkthrough.text: Set up the game exactly as described in the setup section. With 3 players, you will have 5 planets in the center. Take a moment to look at each planet card -- notice the VP value, the special action text, and whether the colony track ends with a Diplomacy or Economy symbol. This preview will help you plan your opening moves.

walkthrough.title: Your First Roll
walkthrough.text: Player 1, pick up your 4 dice and roll them! Look at what you got. You might see a mix of Move, Energy, Culture, Diplomacy, Economy, and Utilize Colony faces. Do not rush to activate -- survey your options first. Which planets look appealing? Do you need resources before moving ships out?

walkthrough.title: First Move -- Send a Ship Out
walkthrough.text: If you rolled a Move die, let's use it first. Pick one of your two ships from your Galaxy Mat and send it to a planet. For your first game, try landing on a planet surface (upright) to see how planet actions work. Read the action text on the planet -- you get to perform it immediately. Place the die in the Activation Bay.

walkthrough.title: Acquire Some Resources
walkthrough.text: If you rolled an Acquire Energy or Acquire Culture die, activate it now. Count how many of your ships are on planets matching that resource type, plus any ships on your Galaxy Mat for Energy. Move your resource token up accordingly. Even gaining 1 or 2 resources early is valuable -- Culture fuels following, and Energy fuels rerolls.

walkthrough.title: Try a Free Reroll
walkthrough.text: If you have unactivated dice with faces you do not need, this is a great time to use your free reroll. Pick up any unactivated dice and roll them again. Remember, this first reroll is free -- additional rerolls this turn will cost 1 Energy each. See if the new faces give you better options.

walkthrough.title: Other Players -- Practice Following
walkthrough.text: Now it is the other players' moment. After Player 1 activates each die, the other players should check: do I want to follow this action? If Player 1 just activated a Move die, any other player can spend 1 Culture to also move one of their ships. This is a core mechanic -- practice it from the very first turn.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake 1: Forgetting to Follow
walkthrough.text: One of the most common beginner mistakes is ignoring the follow opportunity. Every time the active player places a die in the Activation Bay, ALL other players should pause and consider whether to follow. If you skip this, you are essentially playing with half the actions available to you. Culture spent on follows is never wasted -- it keeps you competitive on every turn, not just your own.

walkthrough.title: Enter Orbit on a Planet
walkthrough.text: On someone's turn, use a Move action to send a ship into orbit around a planet. Lay the ship on its side on the first space of the colony track. Note the symbol at the end of the track -- is it Diplomacy or Economy? That tells you which die face you will need to advance along the track. Orbiting is how you eventually colonize planets for big VP.

walkthrough.title: Advance Along a Colony Track
walkthrough.text: When you roll a Diplomacy or Economy die that matches your orbiting planet's colony track, activate it to advance your ship one space forward. Each space you advance brings you closer to colonization. If multiple ships are orbiting different planets of the same type, you only advance one ship per die activation.

walkthrough.title: Race to Colonize
walkthrough.text: Notice that other players may also have ships on colony tracks. The first player to reach the end colonizes the planet -- everyone else's ships get sent home. This creates exciting races. If you see an opponent close to finishing a track, you might want to prioritize advancing your own ship or redirect your efforts elsewhere.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake 2: Ignoring Empire Upgrades
walkthrough.text: New players often focus entirely on colonizing planets and forget to upgrade their empire. Upgrading is one of the most powerful actions in the game -- it gives you more dice per turn, unlocks additional ships, and earns VP by itself. Spending 3 Culture or 3 Energy to reach empire level 3 (gaining an extra die and more VP) is almost always worth it in the early game. Use the Utilize a Colony die to trigger your Galaxy Mat action.

walkthrough.title: Upgrade Your Empire
walkthrough.text: Let's practice an upgrade. When you roll a Utilize Colony die, choose to perform your Galaxy Mat action. You need to pay resources equal to the next level number -- entirely in Energy or entirely in Culture. For example, upgrading from level 1 to level 2 costs 2 Energy or 2 Culture. After upgrading, move your Empire token forward. If you unlocked a new ship, place it on your Galaxy Mat immediately -- you can use it this turn!

walkthrough.title: Use the Converter
walkthrough.text: Now try the Converter. If you have at least 3 unactivated dice and need a specific action, place 2 dice into the Converter slots on the Control Mat. They are now spent. Then take a third unactivated die and set it to whichever face you want. This can only be done once per turn, so save it for when you really need a specific action.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake 3: Poor Converter Usage
walkthrough.text: Beginners often either forget the Converter exists or use it wastefully. Sacrificing 2 dice to change 1 die is a steep cost -- you are giving up 2 actions to guarantee 1. The Converter is best used when you absolutely need a specific die face to colonize a planet, complete a crucial upgrade, or prevent an opponent from winning. Do not use it just because you have unexciting dice -- sometimes activating mediocre dice is better than losing two of them.

walkthrough.title: Colonize Your First Planet
walkthrough.text: By now, someone should be close to the end of a colony track. Keep advancing with the right die type. When you reach the final space, congratulations -- you have colonized a planet! All ships on that card return to owners' mats. Slide the planet card under your Galaxy Mat with the text and VP visible. A replacement planet is drawn from the deck. Announce your new VP total to the table.

walkthrough.title: Use a Colonized Planet's Action
walkthrough.text: Now that you own a colonized planet, the Utilize Colony die becomes even more powerful. Instead of just upgrading your empire, you can choose to perform the special action printed on any colonized planet. Some planet actions let you acquire extra resources, move ships, or advance on colony tracks. The more planets you colonize, the more options Utilize Colony gives you.

walkthrough.title: Keep an Eye on the VP Threshold
walkthrough.text: As the game progresses, watch everyone's VP total. The endgame triggers at 21 VP. Points come from your empire level and colonized planets. Secret Mission VP do NOT count toward triggering the endgame -- they only apply during final scoring. If an opponent is close to 21, you may need to rush your own scoring or try to block their colonization.

walkthrough.title: Endgame and Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: When someone hits 21 VP, finish the round so all players have equal turns. Then reveal Secret Missions. If you completed your mission objective, add those bonus VP. Tally everything: empire level VP + colonized planet VP + secret mission VP. The commander with the most points rules the galaxy!

walkthrough.title: Reflect on Your First Game
walkthrough.text: Take a moment to discuss the game. Did you follow enough? Did you upgrade early enough? Did the Converter help at a critical moment? Most first-time players discover that following is far more important than they expected, and that early empire upgrades create a snowball advantage. With these lessons, you are ready to chart your own course through the cosmos.

walkthrough.title: Launch Your Next Mission
walkthrough.text: You have completed your training mission, Commander! You now understand dice activation, following, colonization races, empire upgrades, and the Converter. In your next game, pay closer attention to your Secret Mission and try to plan around it from the start. The galaxy is vast, and every game creates a different constellation of planets and opportunities. Ready your fleet -- the stars are calling!

bullets: Best at 2-3 players for learning
~25 minutes per game at 2 players

walkthrough.title: Follow Early and Often
walkthrough.text: Culture spent on following is the best investment in the game. On other players' turns, you are essentially getting free actions for just 1 Culture each. In a 4-player game, you have three other turns to follow between each of your own. Players who follow aggressively consistently outpace those who hoard Culture. Make acquiring Culture a priority so you always have fuel for follows.

walkthrough.title: Upgrade Your Empire Early
walkthrough.text: Getting from empire level 1 to level 2 or 3 as quickly as possible gives you more dice and potentially more ships. More dice means more actions per turn and more options. The VP from empire levels also adds up -- at level 4, your empire alone is worth 5 VP. Do not wait until mid-game to start upgrading; the earlier you invest, the more turns you benefit from the extra dice.

walkthrough.title: Balance Surface Landings and Orbiting
walkthrough.text: Landing on a planet's surface gives you an immediate one-time action, while orbiting starts the colonization process for permanent VP and a reusable action. New players often do too much of one and not enough of the other. Use surface landings for quick resource boosts or emergency actions, and orbiting for long-term planet acquisition. A good rhythm is to have some ships working toward colonization while others hop between surfaces for tactical advantages.

walkthrough.title: Keep Resources Flowing
walkthrough.text: Running out of Energy means you cannot reroll, and running out of Culture means you cannot follow. Both are crippling. Try to maintain at least 1-2 Energy and 1-2 Culture as a safety net. Position ships on resource-producing planets so that Acquire dice are never wasted. Ships sitting on your Galaxy Mat always produce Energy, which makes Energy slightly easier to maintain than Culture.

walkthrough.title: Choose Planets Strategically
walkthrough.text: Not all planets are created equal. Consider the VP value, the colony track length, and the special action. Short colony tracks (2-3 spaces) are faster to colonize but usually worth fewer VP. Longer tracks are more valuable but risky -- opponents might beat you there. Also consider whether the colony track uses Diplomacy or Economy, and pick planets that align with the dice you tend to roll or the actions you plan to follow.

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Opponents
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to where other players' ships are orbiting and how far along colony tracks they are. If someone is one space from colonizing a planet you also want, you need to decide: race them for it or redirect to an uncontested planet. Also watch their resource levels -- a player with zero Culture cannot follow you, which is a good time to make aggressive moves.

walkthrough.title: Plan Around Your Secret Mission
walkthrough.text: Your Secret Mission can be worth significant bonus VP at endgame. Read it carefully during setup and let it guide your decisions. If your mission rewards colonizing a certain type of planet or reaching a specific empire level, factor that into your strategy from the beginning. But do not tunnel-vision on it -- if the mission is not achievable, focus on maximizing points through other means.

walkthrough.title: Know When to Trigger the Endgame
walkthrough.text: Reaching 21 VP triggers the endgame, but all players finish the round. If you are ahead, trigger it as soon as possible to lock in your lead. If you are behind, try to slow the leader by competing for the same planets or denying them colony track advancement through strategic following. Remember that Secret Mission VP do not count toward the 21 VP trigger, so a player at 18 VP with a secret mission could actually end up with 22+ in final scoring.

summary.title: Follow Early and Often
bullets: Following gives you actions on opponents' turns for just 1 Culture
Prioritize acquiring Culture to fuel follows

summary.title: Upgrade Your Empire Early
bullets: More dice = more actions and options per turn
Empire levels themselves are worth VP; invest early

summary.title: Balance Surface Landings and Orbiting
bullets: Surface = immediate action; Orbit = colonization and permanent VP
Mix both: colonize for long-term, surface-hop for tactical gains

summary.title: Keep Resources Flowing
bullets: Maintain 1-2 Energy (for rerolls) and 1-2 Culture (for follows)
Galaxy Mat ships always produce Energy; position ships on Culture planets

summary.title: Choose Planets Strategically
bullets: Weigh VP value vs. colony track length vs. special action
Match colony track type (Diplomacy/Economy) to your dice tendencies

summary.title: Watch Your Opponents
bullets: Track opponents' colony progress and resource levels
Exploit low-Culture opponents who cannot follow your moves

summary.title: Plan Around Your Secret Mission
bullets: Read mission during setup; let it guide early decisions
Do not tunnel-vision -- pivot if mission is unachievable

summary.title: Know When to Trigger the Endgame
bullets: Trigger at 21 VP when ahead to lock in your lead
Secret Mission VP do not count toward the 21 VP trigger

walkthrough.title: The Follow Economy Engine
walkthrough.text: At higher levels of play, Culture is the most important resource in the game. Build a strategy around acquiring Culture and spending it aggressively on follows. In a 4-player game, following 2-3 times per round effectively triples your action count compared to only acting on your own turn. Target Culture-producing planets early, and consider colonizing planets whose actions grant Culture. The player who follows most consistently usually wins.

walkthrough.title: Rush Empire Level 3
walkthrough.text: Empire level 3 is the sweet spot where you unlock your 5th die and 3rd ship. Going from 4 dice to 5 dice is a 25% increase in actions per turn, and the additional ship gives you more board presence. Prioritize this upgrade in the first few rounds, even if it means delaying colonization. The compounding advantage of an extra die over the rest of the game far outweighs the cost of 3 resources.

walkthrough.title: Colony Track Blocking
walkthrough.text: If you see an opponent close to colonizing a high-VP planet, consider entering orbit on that same planet. Even if you cannot win the race, your presence on the track creates tension -- the opponent might have to divert dice to advancing faster, or you might get lucky with follows and beat them. At minimum, you deny them a stress-free colonization. This is especially effective on long colony tracks.

walkthrough.title: Surface Hopping for Maximum Extraction
walkthrough.text: Advanced players use Move dice to rapidly hop between planet surfaces, chaining powerful planet actions without committing to colonization. If the available planets have strong surface actions (resource generation, free advancement, etc.), a ship can visit multiple planets over consecutive turns. This strategy works best when you have extra ships from empire upgrades, letting some ships surface-hop while others work colony tracks.

walkthrough.title: Converter Timing Mastery
walkthrough.text: The Converter is most powerful in two specific situations: finishing a colonization race on the same turn an opponent threatens to complete theirs, and executing a double upgrade on a single turn. Sacrifice two low-value dice (like redundant resource dice when your resources are full) to guarantee the exact die face you need. Experienced players save the Converter for turns where it creates a 3+ VP swing.

walkthrough.title: Utilize Colony Chains
walkthrough.text: As you colonize planets, the Utilize Colony die grows in power. With 3+ colonized planets, you have significant flexibility -- each Utilize Colony die can trigger any one of your colony actions or your empire upgrade. Plan your colonization to build a diverse portfolio of actions: one planet for resources, one for ship movement, one for colony advancement. This turns every Utilize Colony roll into a Swiss army knife.

walkthrough.title: Deny Follow Opportunities
walkthrough.text: If you notice that your opponents have high Culture reserves and are following you every activation, consider the order and timing of your dice. Activate dice that benefit you but are less useful to opponents first. If an opponent has no ships available for a Move action, activating Move when they cannot benefit denies them value. Conversely, save your most follow-worthy dice (like Diplomacy or Economy on contested tracks) for last, when opponents may have already spent their Culture.

walkthrough.title: Secret Mission Drafting
walkthrough.text: When you receive your two Secret Mission cards at setup, do not just pick the one that sounds easiest. Consider which mission has the highest VP ceiling, which one aligns with the visible planet layout, and which one your opponents are unlikely to block. Some missions reward colonizing specific planet types -- check how many of those are currently in play. If neither mission fits the board state, pick the one with the lower difficulty as a safety net.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Acceleration vs. VP Padding
walkthrough.text: When you are approaching 21 VP, you face a choice: trigger the endgame immediately, or spend one more turn padding your score. If other players are close behind, trigger immediately -- giving them another full round is dangerous. If you have a comfortable lead, one extra turn of colonizing or upgrading might push your final score even higher. Factor in opponents' Secret Missions: if someone at 16 VP might have a 5-point mission, they could still beat you.

walkthrough.title: Resource Starvation Tactics
walkthrough.text: Advanced players monitor opponents' resources closely. An opponent at 0 Culture cannot follow any of your actions, making your turn effectively uncontested. An opponent at 0 Energy cannot reroll, making their turns more luck-dependent. If you can time your big moves for when opponents are resource-starved, you gain a massive advantage. This awareness turns Tiny Epic Galaxies from a dice game into a strategic duel.

summary.title: The Follow Economy Engine
bullets: Culture is the most valuable resource at high-level play
Following 2-3 times per round can triple your effective action count

summary.title: Rush Empire Level 3
bullets: 5th die = 25% more actions per turn; 3rd ship = more board presence
Prioritize this over early colonization for compounding returns

summary.title: Colony Track Blocking
bullets: Enter orbit on contested planets to pressure opponents
Even losing a race costs the leader extra dice to outpace you

summary.title: Surface Hopping for Maximum Extraction
bullets: Rapidly move between surfaces to chain planet actions
Best with extra ships: some hop surfaces, others colonize

summary.title: Converter Timing Mastery
bullets: Best for finishing colonization races or double upgrades
Save for 3+ VP swing moments; sacrifice low-value dice

summary.title: Utilize Colony Chains
bullets: Colonize diverse planets for flexible Utilize Colony options
3+ colonies turn every Utilize roll into a Swiss army knife

summary.title: Deny Follow Opportunities
bullets: Activate dice opponents cannot benefit from first
Save high-value dice for last when opponents may be out of Culture

summary.title: Secret Mission Drafting
bullets: Evaluate missions against the visible planet layout
Pick highest VP ceiling that aligns with board state

summary.title: Endgame Acceleration vs. VP Padding
bullets: Trigger endgame immediately when opponents are close behind
Factor in hidden Secret Mission VP when evaluating opponent threats

summary.title: Resource Starvation Tactics
bullets: Time big moves when opponents are at 0 Culture or 0 Energy
0 Culture = cannot follow; 0 Energy = cannot reroll

entries.definition: Dice face that lets you relocate one ship from your Galaxy Mat or any planet to a different planet. You choose to land on the surface (upright) or enter orbit (on side).
Dice face that grants 1 Energy for each of your ships on or orbiting Energy-producing planets, plus 1 Energy per ship on your Galaxy Mat.
Dice face that grants 1 Culture for each of your ships on or orbiting Culture-producing planets. Ships on your Galaxy Mat do NOT produce Culture.
Dice face that advances one of your orbiting ships one space forward on a colony track that ends with the Diplomacy symbol.
Dice face that advances one of your orbiting ships one space forward on a colony track that ends with the Economy symbol.
Dice face that lets you perform either your Galaxy Mat action (upgrade empire) or the special action on one of your colonized planets.
One of two resources in the game, tracked on your Galaxy Mat resource track (max 7). Spent on paid rerolls (1 per reroll after the first free one) and empire upgrades.
One of two resources in the game, tracked on your Galaxy Mat resource track (max 7). Spent to follow other players' dice activations (1 per follow) and empire upgrades.
Your personal player board showing the empire track, resource track, ship track, and your Galaxy Mat action (upgrade empire). Ships on your Galaxy Mat produce Energy but not Culture.
The shared central board with the Activation Bay (where used dice are placed) and the Converter (where dice are sacrificed to change another die's face).
The area on the Control Mat where dice are placed when activated. Once a die is in the Activation Bay, it is spent and cannot be used again that turn.
A section on the Control Mat that accepts 2 unactivated dice as a sacrifice, allowing you to set 1 other unactivated die to any face. Usable once per turn. Converted dice cannot be followed.
Track on your Galaxy Mat showing your current empire level. Higher levels grant more dice per turn, more ships, and more victory points. Starts at level 1 (star icon).
Track on your Galaxy Mat for tracking Energy and Culture levels. Each resource ranges from 0 to 7. Tokens are placed on the track to show current amounts.
Track on your Galaxy Mat where locked ships wait to be unlocked via empire upgrades. Ships on squared number spaces are released when you reach the corresponding empire level.
The track on each Planet Card showing spaces a ship must advance through to colonize. Ends with either a Diplomacy or Economy symbol indicating which die type advances ships.
Cards in the center of the table representing planets available for colonization. Each shows a VP value, a special action (usable when landing on surface or after colonizing), and a colony track.
The main area of a Planet Card. Landing a ship here (upright) lets you immediately perform the planet's action. Max 1 of your ships per surface.
Placing a ship on a colony track (lying on side) to begin working toward colonization. Max 1 of your ships per colony track, but multiple players can share the same track.

When a ship reaches the final space of a colony track, that player colonizes the planet. All ships on the card return home. The planet is placed under the colonizer's Galaxy Mat for permanent VP and its action.
After the active player activates a die, each other player may spend 1 Culture to copy that action. Each player may follow at most once per die activation. Timing disputes resolve clockwise.
Rerolling any number of unactivated dice. The first reroll per turn is free; each additional reroll costs 1 Energy. Dice in the Activation Bay cannot be rerolled.

The Galaxy Mat action triggered by Utilize Colony. Pay resources equal to the next empire level cost, entirely in Energy or entirely in Culture (no mixing). Increases VP, dice count, and may unlock ships.
A hidden objective card slid under your Galaxy Mat during setup. Awards bonus VP at end of game if completed. Secret Mission VP do NOT count toward triggering the 21 VP endgame threshold.

The measure of success. Earned from empire level and colonized planets during the game, plus Secret Mission bonuses at endgame. First to 21 VP triggers endgame; highest total after final scoring wins.

When any player reaches 21 or more VP (not counting Secret Missions), the end of game begins. Play continues until all players have had equal turns, then Secret Missions are revealed and final scores tallied.
If players tie on VP, the player with the most colonized planets wins. If still tied: highest empire level. If still tied: highest combined resources. If still tied: shared victory.
The player whose turn it currently is. The active player rolls and activates dice. All other players may follow each die activation by spending Culture.
If at any point during the game your ship is displaced from its position (e.g., when a planet is colonized by another player), it is returned to your Galaxy Mat.

The solo play opponent printed on the back of Galaxy Mats. Uses different rules: moves ships to leftmost open planet, advances all ships of matching type at once, and has special attack actions at higher empire levels.
